cluster_fsm.spec_reference: Matter 1.5 Application Cluster Specification, Section 4.3, Pages 327-373

features.name: Heating
features.description: Thermostat is capable of managing a heating device

features.name: Cooling
features.description: Thermostat is capable of managing a cooling device

features.name: ScheduleConfiguration
features.description: Supports remote configuration of a weekly schedule of setpoint transitions

features.name: Setback
features.description: Supports configurable setback (or span) around setpoints to reduce over-cycling

features.name: AutoMode
features.description: Supports a System Mode of Auto where both heating and cooling demands are generated as needed

features.name: LocalTemperatureNotExposed
features.description: Thermostat does not expose the LocalTemperature value; LocalTemperature attribute always reports null

features.name: MatterScheduleConfiguration
features.description: Supports enhanced schedules with ScheduleStruct; requires time synchronization mechanism

dependencies.description: Temperature Measurement client cluster MAY be on the same endpoint for remote temperature sensing (Section 4.3.7)
Occupancy Sensing client cluster MAY be on the same endpoint for remote occupancy sensing (Section 4.3.7)

states.name: Mode_Off
states.description: Thermostat does not generate demand for Cooling or Heating. No HVAC equipment is commanded to run.
states.safety_implications: HVAC equipment is inactive. Risk of environment reaching unsafe temperatures if thermostat stays in Off and building automation depends on it.

states.name: Mode_Heat
states.description: Demand is only generated for Heating. Thermostat commands heating equipment when Calculated Local Temperature is below the applicable heating setpoint.

state_attributes.ThermostatRunningMode: Heat (when actively heating) or Off (when at setpoint)

states.safety_implications: Heating equipment is driven; risk of overheating if setpoints are set too high or if EmergencyHeat is triggered inadvertently. PII concern: Heating patterns reveal occupancy.

states.name: Mode_Cool
states.description: Demand is only generated for Cooling. Thermostat commands cooling equipment when Calculated Local Temperature is above the applicable cooling setpoint.

state_attributes.ThermostatRunningMode: Cool (when actively cooling) or Off (when at setpoint)

states.safety_implications: Cooling equipment is driven. Risk of overcooling (e.g., cold stress in homes with vulnerable occupants).

states.name: Mode_Auto
states.description: Demand is generated for either Cooling or Heating as required. Thermostat automatically switches between heating and cooling based on temperature relative to both setpoints.

state_attributes.ThermostatRunningMode: Heat (when below heat setpoint), Cool (when above cool setpoint), Off (when between setpoints)

states.safety_implications: Complex mode; if deadband is not enforced, thermostat could simultaneously command heating and cooling equipment, causing equipment damage or energy waste.

states.name: Mode_EmergencyHeat
states.description: 2nd stage heating is in use to achieve desired temperature rapidly. Activated manually or automatically when temperature deficit exceeds EmergencyHeatDelta.
states.safety_implications: HIGH RISK: Highest stage of heating is engaged. Risk of excessive energy consumption, equipment overload, or fire hazard if EmergencyHeat triggers incorrectly or remains active after setpoint is reached.

states.name: Mode_Precooling
states.description: Pre-cooling mode before a scheduled cooling event to prepare the space.
states.safety_implications: Cooling equipment is actively running before needed. Risk of over-cooling if started too early.

states.name: Mode_FanOnly
states.description: Only the fan is running; no heating or cooling demand is generated.
states.safety_implications: Minimal physical risk; fan circulates air without heating or cooling.

states.name: Mode_Dry
states.description: Dehumidification mode; removes humidity without significant temperature change.
states.safety_implications: Dehumidifier operation; low risk unless drainage fails.

states.name: Mode_Sleep
states.description: Sleep mode; may apply energy-saving setpoints appropriate for sleeping occupants.

state_attributes.ThermostatRunningMode: Heat or Cool depending on temperature

states.safety_implications: Occupants may be in reduced-awareness state; setpoint adjustments here could affect comfort and health without occupants realizing.

transitions.guard_condition: SystemMode_new == Off
Feature_HEAT == true && ControlSequenceOfOperation IN [HeatingOnly, HeatingWithReheat, CoolingAndHeating, CoolingAndHeatingWithReheat] && SystemMode_new == Heat

error_responses.description: Heat mode not supported without HEAT feature

error_responses.condition: ControlSequenceOfOperation NOT IN [HeatingOnly, HeatingWithReheat, CoolingAndHeating, CoolingAndHeatingWithReheat]
error_responses.description: SystemMode value not consistent with ControlSequenceOfOperation

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_COOL == true && ControlSequenceOfOperation IN [CoolingOnly, CoolingWithReheat, CoolingAndHeating, CoolingAndHeatingWithReheat] && SystemMode_new == Cool

error_responses.description: Cool mode not supported without COOL feature
Cooling mode not possible when ControlSequenceOfOperation restricts to heating only

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_AUTO == true && ControlSequenceOfOperation IN [CoolingAndHeating, CoolingAndHeatingWithReheat] && SystemMode_new == Auto

error_responses.description: Auto mode not supported without AUTO feature
Auto mode requires ControlSequenceOfOperation supporting both heating and cooling

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_HEAT == true && ControlSequenceOfOperation IN [HeatingOnly, HeatingWithReheat, CoolingAndHeating, CoolingAndHeatingWithReheat] && SystemMode_new == EmergencyHeat

error_responses.description: EmergencyHeat not supported without HEAT feature

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_COOL == true && ControlSequenceOfOperation IN [CoolingOnly, CoolingWithReheat, CoolingAndHeating, CoolingAndHeatingWithReheat] && SystemMode_new == Precooling

error_responses.description: Precooling not supported without COOL feature

transitions.guard_condition: SystemMode_new == FanOnly
SystemMode_new == Dry
SystemMode_new == Sleep
Mode == Heat && Feature_HEAT == true

error_responses.description: Heat mode not supported on this server

transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.1 SetpointRaiseLower Command; Section 4.3.10.1.3 Effect on Receipt

transitions.guard_condition: Mode == Cool && Feature_COOL == true

error_responses.description: Cool mode not supported on this server

transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.1 SetpointRaiseLower Command; Section 4.3.10.1.3 Effect on Receipt

transitions.guard_condition: Mode == Both && Feature_AUTO == true
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.1.1.3 Both Value; Section 4.3.10.1.3 Effect on Receipt - server adjusts only supported setpoints

transitions.guard_condition: Mode == Both && Feature_AUTO == true && resulting_setpoint_invalid_due_to_deadband == true
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.1.1.1 Heat Value; Section 4.3.10.1.1.2 Cool Value - deadband enforcement when AUTO feature supported

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_SCH == true && setpoints_in_range == true && no_overlapping_transitions == true && format_valid == true && total_transitions_within_capacity == true

error_responses.description: Device does not have capacity for total number of transitions requested
Setpoints out of absolute limits or mode not supported; no setpoints from entire sequence applied
Two transitions on the same day with the same time detected

error_responses.condition: device_no_multi_day_support && DayOfWeekForSequence has multiple bits set || device_no_multi_mode_support && ModeForSequence has multiple bits set || Transitions count mismatch
error_responses.description: Command format not supported by this device or payload inconsistency

transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.2 SetWeeklySchedule Command; Section 4.3.10.2.5 Effect on Receipt

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_SCH == true && request_format_valid == true

error_responses.description: Device does not support multi-day or multi-mode requests

transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.3 GetWeeklySchedule Command; Section 4.3.10.3.3 Effect on Receipt

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_SCH == true
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.5 ClearWeeklySchedule Command - no error responses defined

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_MSCH == true && Schedules contains ScheduleStruct with ScheduleHandle == request.ScheduleHandle

error_responses.condition: Schedules does NOT contain ScheduleStruct with matching ScheduleHandle
error_responses.description: Provided ScheduleHandle does not match any existing schedule

transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.6 SetActiveScheduleRequest Command; Section 4.3.10.6.2 Effect on Receipt

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PRES == true && request.PresetHandle == null
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.7 SetActivePresetRequest Command; Section 4.3.10.7.2 Effect on Receipt, step 1

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PRES == true && request.PresetHandle != null && Presets contains PresetStruct with PresetHandle == request.PresetHandle

error_responses.condition: request.PresetHandle != null AND Presets does NOT contain PresetStruct with matching PresetHandle
error_responses.description: Provided PresetHandle does not match any existing preset

transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.10.7 SetActivePresetRequest Command; Section 4.3.10.7.2 Effect on Receipt, step 2

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_COOL == true && value >= MinCoolSetpointLimit && value <= MaxCoolSetpointLimit && value >= (OccupiedHeatingSetpoint + MinSetpointDeadBand)
actions: if (Feature_PRES == true && (Feature_OCC == false || Occupancy.Occupied == true)) ActivePresetHandle := null

error_responses.description: Value outside user-configurable cooling setpoint limits

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_COOL == true && value >= MinCoolSetpointLimit && value <= MaxCoolSetpointLimit && value < (OccupiedHeatingSetpoint + MinSetpointDeadBand)
actions: if (Feature_PRES == true && (Feature_OCC == false || Occupancy.Occupied == true)) ActivePresetHandle := null

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_HEAT == true && value >= MinHeatSetpointLimit && value <= MaxHeatSetpointLimit && value <= (OccupiedCoolingSetpoint - MinSetpointDeadBand)
actions: if (Feature_PRES == true && (Feature_OCC == false || Occupancy.Occupied == true)) ActivePresetHandle := null

error_responses.description: Value outside user-configurable heating setpoint limits

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_HEAT == true && value >= MinHeatSetpointLimit && value <= MaxHeatSetpointLimit && value > (OccupiedCoolingSetpoint - MinSetpointDeadBand)
actions: if (Feature_PRES == true && (Feature_OCC == false || Occupancy.Occupied == true)) ActivePresetHandle := null

transitions.guard_condition: value == SetpointHoldOn && TemperatureSetpointHoldDuration != null && SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp is supported
transitions.timing_constraint: Duration in minutes specified by TemperatureSetpointHoldDuration attribute

transitions.guard_condition: value == SetpointHoldOn && (TemperatureSetpointHoldDuration == null || SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp not supported)
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.29 TemperatureSetpointHold Attribute; Section 4.3.9.61 SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp Attribute

transitions.guard_condition: value == SetpointHoldOff
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.29 TemperatureSetpointHold Attribute; Section 4.3.9.61 SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp Attribute

transitions.guard_condition: TemperatureSetpointHold == SetpointHoldOn && current_utc_time >= SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp
transitions.timing_constraint: Autonomous expiry when SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp is reached
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.61 SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp Attribute - 'known time when TemperatureSetpointHold SHALL be cleared'

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_HEAT == true && SystemMode IN [Heat, Auto] && (OccupiedHeatingSetpoint - CalculatedLocalTemperature) >= EmergencyHeatDelta
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.42 EmergencyHeatDelta Attribute - 'SHALL immediately switch to the SystemMode value that provides the highest stage of heating'

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_HEAT == true && SystemMode == Auto && (OccupiedHeatingSetpoint - CalculatedLocalTemperature) >= EmergencyHeatDelta
Feature_MSCH == true && (TemperatureSetpointHold == SetpointHoldOff || ThermostatProgrammingOperationMode.ScheduleActive == false) == false && active_schedule_transition_found == true
actions: if (Feature_COOL && Feature_OCC && Occupancy.Occupied == false) UnoccupiedCoolingSetpoint := transition.CoolingSetpoint else OccupiedCoolingSetpoint := transition.CoolingSetpoint
if (Feature_HEAT && Feature_OCC && Occupancy.Occupied == false) UnoccupiedHeatingSetpoint := transition.HeatingSetpoint else OccupiedHeatingSetpoint := transition.HeatingSetpoint
transitions.timing_constraint: Triggered at TransitionTime (minutes since midnight) on matching DayOfWeek
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.8.27.5 Transitions Field; Section 4.3.8.28 ScheduleTransitionStruct Type

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_SB == true && value >= OccupiedSetbackMin && value <= OccupiedSetbackMax
Feature_SB == true && value > OccupiedSetbackMax
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.36 OccupiedSetback Attribute - 'SHALL set its OccupiedSetback value to OccupiedSetbackMax and SHALL send SUCCESS'

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_SB == true && value < OccupiedSetbackMin
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.36 OccupiedSetback Attribute - 'SHALL set its OccupiedSetback value to OccupiedSetbackMin and SHALL send SUCCESS'

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_SB == true && Feature_OCC == true && value > UnoccupiedSetbackMax
Feature_LTNE == false
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.12 LocalTemperatureCalibration Attribute - 'SHALL respond with SUCCESS and set value to upper or lower limit reached'

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_LTNE == true && value.LocalTemperature == 1

error_responses.condition: Feature_LTNE == true AND LocalTemperature bit set to 1
error_responses.description: Cannot set LocalTemperature RemoteSensing bit to 1 when LTNE feature is present

transitions.guard_condition: true
actions: return SUCCESS (value unchanged, write silently ignored)
transitions.spec_reference: Section 4.3.9.23 ControlSequenceOfOperation Attribute - 'server SHALL silently ignore the write'

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_HEAT == true && value >= AbsMinHeatSetpointLimit && value <= MaxHeatSetpointLimit && (value <= MinCoolSetpointLimit - MinSetpointDeadBand || Feature_AUTO == false)

error_responses.condition: cannot resolve constraint conflicts with setpoints
error_responses.description: Value not consistent with setpoint limits and conflicts cannot be resolved by adjusting setpoints

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PRES == true && PresetHandle == null && BuiltIn != true && PresetScenario in PresetTypes && total_presets < NumberOfPresets && presets_for_scenario < PresetTypeStruct.NumberOfPresets && name_unique_within_scenario == true

error_responses.description: Cannot create a built-in preset via write
Specified scenario not supported by this thermostat

error_responses.condition: Name set but PresetTypeStruct does not have SupportsNames bit set
error_responses.description: This preset scenario does not support user-provided names
Maximum total preset count exceeded
Maximum preset count for this scenario exceeded

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PRES == true && PresetHandle != null && Presets contains matching PresetHandle && BuiltIn consistency maintained

error_responses.condition: PresetHandle not found in existing presets
error_responses.description: No existing preset with the provided PresetHandle
Duplicate PresetHandle values in the write payload

error_responses.condition: BuiltIn field mismatch with existing preset
error_responses.description: Cannot change BuiltIn status of an existing preset

trigger.name: Presets commit - remove built-in or referenced preset

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_PRES == true && commit attempted && removed_preset.BuiltIn == true OR removed_preset.PresetHandle == ActivePresetHandle OR (Feature_MSCH && removed_preset.PresetHandle referenced in Schedules)
actions: return attribute_status CONSTRAINT_ERROR or INVALID_IN_STATE (commit rejected)

error_responses.description: Cannot delete a built-in preset
Cannot delete the currently active preset

error_responses.condition: removed PresetHandle referenced by a ScheduleTransitionStruct
error_responses.description: Cannot delete a preset that is referenced by an active schedule

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_MSCH == true && ScheduleHandle == null && BuiltIn != true && SystemMode in ScheduleTypes && transitions_within_limits == true && no_duplicate_transitions == true && preset_references_valid == true

error_responses.description: Cannot create a built-in schedule via write
Specified SystemMode not supported for schedules
Too many transitions in this schedule
Too many transitions on a single day

error_responses.condition: PresetHandle present but ScheduleTypeStruct.SupportsPresets is false
error_responses.description: This schedule type does not support preset handles

error_responses.condition: PresetHandle present but not found in Presets after applying pending changes
error_responses.description: Referenced preset does not exist
Maximum schedule count reached

trigger.name: Schedules commit - remove built-in or active schedule

transitions.guard_condition: Feature_MSCH == true && commit attempted && (removed_schedule.BuiltIn == true OR removed_schedule.ScheduleHandle == ActiveScheduleHandle)
actions: return attribute_status CONSTRAINT_ERROR or INVALID_IN_STATE (commit rejected)

error_responses.description: Cannot delete a built-in schedule
Cannot delete the currently active schedule

transitions.guard_condition: value != null && TemperatureSetpointHold == SetpointHoldOn && SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp is supported
value == null && SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp is supported

timers.name: SetpointHoldExpiryTimer
timers.trigger: TemperatureSetpointHold written to SetpointHoldOn when TemperatureSetpointHoldDuration is non-null; or when TemperatureSetpointHoldDuration is updated to a non-null value while TemperatureSetpointHold is SetpointHoldOn

timers.name: ScheduleTransitionTimer
timers.trigger: Wall clock time matches a TransitionTime (minutes since midnight) on a matching DayOfWeek in the active schedule, and TemperatureSetpointHold is SetpointHoldOff and ScheduleActive bit is set in ThermostatProgrammingOperationMode
timers.resolution: minutes (TransitionTime in minutes since midnight, max 1439)

functions.name: clamp
functions.description: Returns value constrained within [min, max]. If value < min, returns min. If value > max, returns max. This clamping is NOT considered an error condition for SetpointRaiseLower.

functions.name: generate_unique_preset_handle
functions.description: Device-generated unique identifier for a new preset. The identifier SHALL be unique on the device and SHALL NOT be reused after the preset is deleted.

functions.name: generate_unique_schedule_handle
functions.description: Device-generated unique identifier for a new schedule. The identifier SHALL be unique on the device and SHALL NOT be reused after the schedule is deleted.

functions.name: weekly_schedule_store
functions.description: Stores the provided weekly schedule transitions in the thermostat, replacing any existing transitions for the specified DayOfWeekForSequence. Multiple commands may be needed for schedules exceeding 10 transitions.

functions.name: weekly_schedule_clear_all
functions.description: Removes all stored weekly schedule transitions from the thermostat. No error conditions defined.

functions.name: apply_schedule_setpoints_from_transition

parameters.name: transition
parameters.description: The active schedule transition to apply

functions.description: Applies setpoints from a ScheduleTransitionStruct using the priority order: (1) PresetHandle if provided, (2) explicit HeatingSetpoint or CoolingSetpoint fields, (3) parent ScheduleStruct PresetHandle, (4) server default. Updates OccupiedCoolingSetpoint, OccupiedHeatingSetpoint, UnoccupiedCoolingSetpoint, UnoccupiedHeatingSetpoint, and ActivePresetHandle as appropriate.

functions.name: adjust_to_maintain_deadband
functions.description: Given a new heating setpoint, returns the minimum cooling setpoint required to maintain the deadband constraint: result = heat_setpoint + deadband. Used when raising heat setpoint in AUTO mode would violate the deadband.

manage_commands: Write ACType, ACCapacity, ACRefrigerantType, ACCompressorType, ACErrorCode, ACLouverPosition, ACCapacityFormat (attributes)

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Autonomous EmergencyHeat activation: when heating deficit >= EmergencyHeatDelta, the thermostat SHALL immediately switch to 2nd stage (emergency) heating. If EmergencyHeatDelta is set very low, this could trigger unnecessarily and consume excessive energy or overheat the space.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: EmergencyHeatDelta is configurable (default 25.5°C) and requires Manage access to modify. Thermostat continues until OccupiedHeatingSetpoint is reached.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Maximum heating power is engaged. Risk of equipment overload, excessive energy consumption, and in extreme cases, fire hazard (e.g., if heating element is defective).
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Requires HEAT feature; can be manually cleared by writing SystemMode. Spec does not define automatic return from EmergencyHeat once setpoint is reached.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: A malicious actor with Manage access could set EmergencyHeatDelta to 0, causing the thermostat to permanently operate in EmergencyHeat mode whenever in a heating mode.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Requires Manage (M) privilege; no minimum value constraint defined in spec.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Setting cooling setpoint extremely low could trigger overcooling, causing cold stress for vulnerable occupants (elderly, infants). Setpoint requires only Operate (O) privilege.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Constrained by MinCoolSetpointLimit and AbsMinCoolSetpointLimit (default 16°C); CONSTRAINT_ERROR returned for out-of-range values.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Setting heating setpoint extremely high could overheat occupants or trigger equipment damage. Setpoint requires only Operate (O) privilege.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Constrained by MaxHeatSetpointLimit and AbsMaxHeatSetpointLimit (default 30°C); CONSTRAINT_ERROR returned for out-of-range values.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: ControlSequenceOfOperation writes are silently ignored. A management system believing it has changed the operating environment may be unaware the thermostat is still operating in a restricted mode, leading to unexpected mode rejections.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Spec explicitly notes silence is for backwards compatibility. Callers should read the attribute back to confirm current value.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: If deadband approaches 0 and the AUTO feature is supported, the thermostat could simultaneously demand heating and cooling, wasting energy or damaging HVAC equipment.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Writes to MinSetpointDeadBand are silently ignored (backwards compatibility); effective range is 0 to 12.7°C. Combined deadband constraints enforced on setpoint pairs.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Schedule-driven setpoint changes occur autonomously without user interaction. Maliciously crafted schedules (by a Manage-level attacker) could override comfort settings at specific times.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: TemperatureSetpointHold=SetpointHoldOn overrides schedules; requires Manage access to write schedules.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Applying a large calibration offset causes the thermostat to make decisions based on a falsely reported temperature, potentially driving the environment to unsafe conditions.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Requires Manage access; clamped to device-supported range (historically -2.5°C to 2.5°C, now wider per Revision 8).

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Disabling ScheduleActive (bit 0) by a remote actor prevents schedule-based energy conservation, or conversely, enabling it could engage an unexpected schedule.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Requires Manage access; attribute is pushing (P) for observability.

spec_gaps.description: No explicit initial/default value for SystemMode is specified in the attribute table. SystemMode=Off is inferred as the safest default but not mandated.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Implementation may power up in an unexpected heating or cooling mode, causing unintended HVAC activation.

spec_gaps.description: Writes to ControlSequenceOfOperation SHALL be silently ignored. The attribute has RW VM access but writes have no effect. A management system that writes this attribute and believes it succeeded may have incorrect assumptions about allowed SystemMode values.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Authorization confusion: a controller with Manage access can write ControlSequenceOfOperation but the value silently does not change, creating a false security assumption about mode restrictions.

spec_gaps.description: MinSetpointDeadBand is declared R[W] (optionally writable) but 'any writes SHALL be silently ignored' for backwards compatibility. This means the attribute has write access advertised but no write effect.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A controller may believe it lowered or raised the deadband without any effect; could be exploited to convince a controller that the deadband is lower than it actually is.

spec_gaps.description: When TemperatureSetpointHold is SetpointHoldOn, the thermostat SHOULD maintain the setpoint, not SHALL. This is a weak normative requirement, allowing implementations to ignore the hold.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A hold intended to prevent schedule override (e.g., for safety) may not be honored by non-compliant implementations.

spec_gaps.description: After SetpointHoldExpiryTimestamp is reached, automatic clearing of TemperatureSetpointHold to SetpointHoldOff is implied but the spec uses 'if there is a known time when...SHALL be cleared' language. The mechanism for automatic hold clearing is not explicitly mandated as a SHALL behavior.
spec_gaps.security_impact: If the hold does not auto-clear, a temporary setpoint hold could become permanent, locking out schedule-driven setpoint adjustment indefinitely.

spec_gaps.description: No minimum value is specified for EmergencyHeatDelta. The default is 25.5°C (maximum for UnsignedTemperature type). A client with Manage access could write 0°C, causing the thermostat to permanently operate in EmergencyHeat when in a heating mode.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Denial of service / excessive energy attack: setting EmergencyHeatDelta to 0 forces continuous emergency heating regardless of temperature, potentially overheating and damaging HVAC equipment.

spec_gaps.description: DST (Daylight Saving Time) handling for schedule transitions is explicitly stated as 'implementation-dependent.' No normative guidance is given on whether transitions should repeat, be skipped, or interpolated during DST changeover.
spec_gaps.security_impact: During DST rollback, a transition time may be ambiguous (executed twice) or, in the spring forward, missed entirely. Could be exploited to prevent expected setpoint changes.

spec_gaps.description: The Vacation bit (bit 7) in ScheduleDayOfWeekBitmap SHALL NOT be set in ScheduleTransitionStruct, but the spec does not define what status code is returned if this constraint is violated (beyond 'SHALL be invalid').
spec_gaps.security_impact: Ambiguity in error response for Vacation bit allows inconsistent implementations; a client may not know the schedule was rejected.

spec_gaps.description: The allowed range for LocalTemperatureCalibration is now implementation-defined (no explicit min/max in the attribute table since Revision 8 removed the -2.5°C to 2.5°C constraint). Devices may expose dangerously large calibration offsets.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A Manage-level attacker can apply a large calibration offset, causing the thermostat's calculated temperature to diverge significantly from reality, driving HVAC to dangerous extremes.

spec_gaps.description: No events (Matter formal event types) are defined for the Thermostat cluster. State changes (SystemMode transitions, setpoint changes, hold activations) are observable only via attribute subscriptions. There is no equivalent of a 'StateChangeEvent' that would provide a reliable audit trail.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Without events, logging and monitoring of thermostat state changes requires polling or subscription management. State changes by a compromised controller may go undetected.

spec_gaps.description: The spec states thermostats with a schedule MAY use this attribute to control schedule usage even without the SCH feature. The interaction between ScheduleActive bit and the MSCH feature schedules is not explicitly defined.
spec_gaps.security_impact: An implementation might ignore the ScheduleActive bit for MSCH schedules, creating a divergence between the programmed intent and actual behavior.

spec_gaps.description: HEAT and COOL features have conformance 'AUTO, O.a+' which means at least one of HEAT or COOL should be present if AUTO is supported, but the exact constraint logic is not fully expressed in the feature bitmap. A device declaring AUTO without either HEAT or COOL is structurally invalid but no error response is defined.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A malformed device could advertise AUTO mode without supporting heating or cooling, leading clients to send SetpointRaiseLower commands that have no physical effect.

notes.all_commands_mapped: SetpointRaiseLower (T010-T013), SetWeeklySchedule (T014), GetWeeklySchedule (T015), ClearWeeklySchedule (T016), SetActiveScheduleRequest (T017), SetActivePresetRequest (T018-T019) mapped. No response-only commands (GetWeeklyScheduleResponse) require separate FSM transitions.
notes.all_events_mapped: Thermostat cluster defines NO formal Matter events (Section 4.3.10 contains no events table). State observability relies on attribute pushing (P quality) and subscriptions. Documented as spec_gap.
notes.timers_modeled: SetpointHoldExpiryTimer (T027) and ScheduleTransitionTimer (T030) are modeled. Weekly schedule timers (SCH feature) operate on wall-clock minutes; MSCH schedule timers operate similarly.
notes.all_features_handled: HEAT, COOL, OCC, SCH, SB, AUTO, LTNE, MSCH, PRES feature guards are applied throughout transitions.
